content: respx for mocking httpx in async pytest tests: respx.mock decorator or context manager intercepts httpx requests at the transport level — no real network calls are made. Key isolation pitfall: if GitHubIntegration creates a new AsyncClient per method call (the per-call pattern), respx.mock works correctly. If a shared AsyncClient is reused at module level, the mock must be applied before the client is instantiated. Another pitfall: tests that don't activate respx will make real HTTP calls if GITHUB_PAT is set in the test environment — always set respx.mock(assert_all_called=False) and ensure GITHUB_PAT is unset or set to a dummy value in test env. Pattern: use pytest.fixture with respx.mock as a context manager; define mock routes for POST /orgs/{org}/teams, POST /orgs/{org}/repos, PUT /orgs/{org}/teams/{slug}/memberships/{username}.
